prompt: 2026 CURRENT pricing + offering for the GRC / compliance-automation leaders: VANTA, DRATA, SECUREFRAME. For each, report (cite URLs/dates): (a) actual pricing if public or the typical annual range buyers report (e.g. Vanta ~$/yr by company size), pricing model (annual subscription, per-framework), (b) what you actually GET (continuous compliance monitoring, SOC 2/ISO automation, trust center, questionnaire automation, any AI/agent security assessment), (c) lead time / time-to-value, (d) do they produce a SIGNED, cryptographically VERIFIABLE evidence artifact a buyer checks offline themselves, or a dashboard/report you trust? (e) do they specifically AUDIT an AI agent's runtime behavior (permissions, egress, injection, what it did) from logs? Be precise + cite real 2026 sources.

2026 CURRENT pricing + offering for security QUESTIONNAIRE AUTOMATION + TRUST CENTER tools: CONVEYOR, SAFEBASE, SKYPHER (and Vanta Questionnaire Automation). For each (cite URLs/dates): (a) pricing if public or typical range + model, (b) what you GET (AI questionnaire auto-answer, trust center / doc portal, self-serve buyer access), (c) do they produce a SIGNED, independently VERIFIABLE security artifact about the vendor's AI, or do they just draft answers / share existing docs (SOC 2 PDF)? (d) do they actually run a security AUDIT of the AI, or just manage/share evidence? Be precise + cite.

2026 CURRENT pricing + lead time for PENETRATION TESTING: traditional pentest/security-audit firms AND pentest-as-a-service (COBALT, HACKERONE, and AI-pentest entrants). Report (cite URLs/@handle+dates): (a) typical cost per engagement (real 2026 numbers, e.g. $30k-$50k+ for a traditional pentest; Cobalt/HackerOne pricing/credits model), (b) lead time / scoping + delivery time (weeks?), (c) the deliverable (a PDF report? retest?), (d) is it on-demand/continuous or a scheduled point-in-time engagement, (e) do they cover AI-AGENT-specific risks (prompt injection, agent permissions, data egress, autonomy) and do they sign/make the report independently verifiable? Quote real founders/buyers on pentest cost + lead-time pain.

2026 CURRENT pricing + offering for AI-SPECIFIC security / red-teaming / governance products: LAKERA, PROTECT AI, HIDDENLAYER, MINDGARD, CREDO AI, CRANIUM, and any 'AI agent security' platforms (Zenity, Operant, Obsidian). For each (cite URLs/dates): (a) pricing if public or model (usually enterprise/annual), (b) what you GET (runtime guardrails/firewall, model scanning, red-teaming, AI governance/risk register, agent security posture), (c) is it a runtime DEFENSE product or does it produce EVIDENCE/an audit a buyer can use to clear a security review, (d) does it produce a SIGNED, offline-VERIFIABLE report, (e) is it on-demand/self-serve or enterprise-sales. Distinguish 'defends the AI at runtime' vs 'produces buyer-verifiable evidence for the sale'.

2026: find the CLOSEST DIRECT competitors to kolm.ai (on-demand, reproducible, SIGNED + offline-verifiable security AUDIT of the AI an enterprise vendor ships, mapped to SOC 2/ISO 42001/NIST AI RMF/EU AI Act/OWASP/MITRE, that the buyer verifies themselves with no kolm account, free scan + $750 signed report + $299-$999/mo continuous + $15k/$25k tiers). Is there anyone doing SIGNED, buyer-verifiable AI security audits/attestations (e.g. Cyphrex, Sigstore-style attestations for AI, any 'AI BOM'/AI attestation startup)? For the COMPETITIVE MATRIX, list the exact comparison dimensions that matter to the buyer (price, lead time, on-demand vs scheduled, AI-agent-specific, signed + offline-verifiable, reproducible/continuous, buyer self-verifies with no account, framework mapping, the actual deliverable). For EACH category (GRC automation, questionnaire/trust center, pentest/PtaaS, AI security/red-team, traditional audit firm), give the one-line summary of price + what you get, and say where kolm uniquely wins and where a competitor is genuinely better. Be specific + cite.
